bio.bio_blurb: Aeronautical engineer; built a supersonic tunnel

narrative_blocks.header: He built America’s first supersonic wind tunnel
narrative_blocks.body: A Harvard-trained aeronautical engineer and Navy man of the Second World War, he was instrumental in establishing the country’s first supersonic wind tunnel at the Aberdeen Proving Grounds. He later served as Assistant Secretary of the Army for Research and Development.

narrative_blocks.header: He helped write the rules of ocean racing
narrative_blocks.body: An avid offshore sailor on the Chesapeake and beyond, he gave years to U.S. Sailing’s measurement committee and chaired the international technical committee of the Ocean Racing Congress — shaping how yacht races worldwide were judged.

narrative_blocks.header: He sang in a group: Bach and Beer
narrative_blocks.body: A lover of choral music, he was an active member of a Washington men’s singing group named Bach and Beer, and chaired the Citizens Association of his Georgetown neighborhood.

bio.bio_blurb: Diplomat’s wife of the Cowles family

narrative_blocks.header: She married a diplomat, then an engineer
narrative_blocks.body: Her first husband, Joseph Greene, gave thirty-two years to the Foreign Service; her second, Charles Poor, helped build America’s first supersonic wind tunnel. She lived to ninety, dying in Washington in 2010.
